matches.moment: Argentina's tournament-leading set-piece threat finally breaches Spain's record defense through a Romero header, but Spain's fresher legs turn Argentina's late commitment forward into a stoppage-time breakaway that settles it.

verdict: Nothing in six weeks of data has suggested this final would be anything but exactly what the fixture list always promised: the tournament's most statistically dominant defense against its most clinically inevitable, if occasionally fortunate, attack. Spain arrive with a defensive record with no historical precedent in the shot-suppression era, 0.31 xG conceded per game, a single goal allowed in seven matches, six clean sheets, built on Rodri's record passing volume and the highest pressing frequency of the tournament. Argentina arrive as the only unbeaten, undefeated, 7-0 side left in the competition, carrying Messi's era-defining farewell run and a tournament-leading habit of scoring 12 of their 19 goals after the 75th minute, but also the worst clean-sheet rate of any side to reach the final four, having conceded to Cape Verde and Egypt alike. The numbers point to a result decided at the margins rather than by a gulf in class: Spain's superior rest, superior underlying defensive solidity, and superior recent scoring against comparable-quality opposition tip a genuinely close final their way, 2-1, with the health warning that Argentina's data-proven gift for finding exactly this kind of goal late has kept the draw scenario alive at 29% right up to kickoff. If Argentina win it, it will look exactly like their last five matches, backs against the wall, then suddenly not. If Spain win it, it will look exactly like their last seven, quiet, suffocating, and settled by the smallest of margins.

players.name: Lionel Messi
players.stat: 6.2 xG, 4 assists (2026), 8 goals, 25 completed dribbles (most by a 30+ player in WC history)
players.watch: A moment of individual creation to break Spain's low-event structure, likely after the 75th minute per his tournament pattern

players.name: Lamine Yamal
players.stat: 3.6 xG, 0.7 xA, 1 goal, 1.82 shots on target per 90
players.watch: Whether the semifinal knock affects his sharpness in one-on-one duels vs. Tagliafico

players.name: Rodri
players.stat: 655 completed passes this tournament, most on record in a single World Cup edition
players.watch: Whether his screening in front of the back four holds for 90-plus minutes against Messi's central pockets

players.name: Mikel Oyarzabal
players.stat: 5 goals this tournament, 3rd Spanish player ever to reach 5 in a single World Cup
players.watch: Continuing his run of scoring in three straight knockout matches

players.name: Cristian Romero
players.stat: Part of Argentina's tournament-leading 5 set-piece goals in 6 games
players.watch: Whether Argentina's best route to goal against Spain's suppression comes from a dead ball rather than open play
